gauge.name: Maries River at Westphalia

impacts.statement: The sewage treatment plant just off of County Road 611 east of Westphalia will begin to flood near this height.
During the flood of August 8, 2013, U.S. Highway 63 was completely inundated at this height just NW of Maries River bridge.
Near this height on 27 December 2015...US Highway 63 was closed.
County Road 611 just west of the bridge begins flooding near this height.
State Highway T will begin flooding southwest of the bridge near this height.
County Road 608 north of the bridge begins flooding near this height.
County Road 512 just south of Westphalia begins flooding near a small creek bridge near this height.
County Road 615 just north of the U.S. Highway 63 bridge begins flooding at this height.
The north end of County Road 521 begins flooding adjacent to the river near this height.
County Road 610 begins flooding near the Indian Creek bridge near this height.

The Sestak Slab bridge along the south portion of County Road 521 (near County Road 522) begins flooding near this height.  This bridge flooded during the crest of 1/30/2013 of 9.76 feet.  It is 11.5 river miles upstream from the gage.

properties.caption: Sestak Slab (CR 521) bridge in December 2012.  This bridge will flood at a stage near 8.2 feet.
County Road 610 near the Bear Creek bridge.  Impact here may occur as low as 9 feet.
State Highway T near Loesch Road (CR 526). This roadway should flood when the river nears 18.3 ft.
County Road 610 near Indian Creek.  This roadway should flood when the river reaches 7-8 feet.
County Road 521 along the Maries River should flood at a stage near 12.1 feet.

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 24 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.
River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 48 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
